prompt: Which of the following is the correct definition of electronegativity?
options: The energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous atom.
The ability of an atom in a bond to attract the shared electrons toward itself.
The energy released when an electron is added to a neutral gaseous atom.
The measure of the total number of electrons in an atom's outermost shell.
hints: Think about what happens to shared electrons in a polar covalent bond.
Electronegativity is a relative measure on a scale (e.g., Pauling scale).
Fluorine has the highest electronegativity of all elements.

prompt: Which type of isomers are 2-butene cis and trans forms?
hints: These isomers have the same molecular formula and connectivity.
They differ in the spatial arrangement around a C=C double bond.
Restricted rotation around the double bond is key.
